answers.a: A metallic strip on a dielectric substrate
answers.b: A narrow slot etched in a conducting plane on a dielectric substrate

explanations.a: A metallic strip on a dielectric substrate describes a microstrip line, not a slotline.
explanations.b: Correct! A slotline consists of a narrow slot etched in a conducting plane on a dielectric substrate.
explanations.c: A coaxial transmission line has an inner and outer conductor, unlike a slotline.
explanations.d: A hollow metallic waveguide is a different type of microwave transmission structure.

questions.question: The conducting strip in a slotline is replaced by:

answers.b: A narrow slot in the ground plane

explanations.a: A dielectric rod is not used in a slotline structure.
explanations.b: Correct! In a slotline, the conducting strip is replaced by a narrow slot in the conducting ground plane.
explanations.c: Two conductors are used in other transmission lines, not slotlines.
explanations.d: A waveguide wall is unrelated to the slotline's conducting structure.

questions.question: Electric field lines in a slotline are concentrated mainly:

explanations.a: A slotline has no central metallic strip for field concentration.
explanations.b: Correct! The electric field is mainly concentrated across the narrow slot.
explanations.c: The fields are not confined only outside the substrate.
explanations.d: A slotline does not contain a coaxial conductor.

questions.question: Which of the following is an application of slotline?

explanations.a: Correct! Slotlines are widely used in microwave antennas and microwave integrated circuits.
explanations.b: Power transformers do not use slotline technology.
explanations.c: DC motors are unrelated to slotline applications.
explanations.d: Audio speakers do not use slotline transmission structures.

questions.question: Which statement about slotline is correct?

answers.a: It consists of two parallel wires.
answers.b: It uses a narrow slot cut in a conducting plane for signal propagation.
answers.c: It is a hollow rectangular waveguide.
answers.d: It cannot be integrated with microwave circuits.

explanations.a: A slotline does not consist of two parallel wires.
explanations.b: Correct! A slotline guides electromagnetic waves using a narrow slot cut in a conducting plane.
explanations.c: A slotline is not a hollow rectangular waveguide.
explanations.d: Slotlines are commonly integrated into microwave circuits.
